mission.title: armada: Build interfaces around our postgres access
mission.description: Build interfaces around our postgres access. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current armada deployment
steps.description:
Verify your armada version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n armada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=armada
helm list -n armada 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working armada installation.

steps.title: Review armada configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant armada configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n armada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=armada
kubectl get configmap -n armada -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=armada
```
We should make sure that users could bring their own SQL databases into Armada.  Currently we only allow postgres as our db but we should create an interface and potentially allow other databases.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Build interfaces around our postgres access
steps.description:
zed with this [Jira Task](https://gr-oss.atlassian.net/browse/BATCH-413) by [Unito](https://www.unito.io)

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n armada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=armada
kubectl get events -n armada --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Build interfaces around our postgres access" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with armada installed or the issue environment reproducible.
